source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Three small equilateral triangles of the same size are cut from the corners of a larger equilateral triangle with sides $6 \mathrm{~cm}$ as shown. The sum of the perimeters of the three small triangles is equal to the perimeter of the remaining hexagon. What is the side-length of one of the small triangles? <image1>

the task depends on a geometric cut-from-corners configuration and the resulting hexagon shape

multi_step: solving requires introducing a side-length variable for the small equilateral triangles
the remaining hexagon perimeter must be expressed from multiple side types created by the cuts
the condition 'sum of the perimeters of the three small triangles equals the perimeter of the remaining hexagon' requires setting up and solving an equation
this is more than direct lookup; it combines diagram interpretation with geometry and algebra

multiple-choice options are provided, giving an additional discrete check
